mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Range Rover Sport P530 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Range Rover Sport P530 (S68B44B) engine typically costs £6,000–£10,000 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 at independent specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Range Rover Sport P530 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The S68B44B is a BMW-sourced 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8 that delivers 530PS and 750Nm of torque. It's a sophisticated engine but has known weak points: the 'Hot V' design generates extreme heat that can cause coolant hoses to become brittle, and a recall was issued for a missing turbo oil drain pipe gasket. Properly maintained, it's a reliable engine; neglected, cooling system repairs can be expensive.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Range Rover Sport P530?

acceptedAnswer.text: 'Hot V' cooling system hose failure (heat causes hoses to become brittle), turbo oil drain pipe gasket failure (recall issue — potential fire risk), engine mount failure (causing vibration at idle), and MHEV starting system issues. All are manageable with proper maintenance and early detection.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover Sport P530 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Against a well-maintained L461 Range Rover Sport (values £50,000–£90,000+), a £7,000–£9,000 reconditioned engine is 10–18% of the vehicle's value — worth it. The Land Rover scrap threshold of 60% means replacement is almost always financially viable on a P530, given the L461's strong residual values.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Range Rover Sport P530 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code S68B44B is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The P530 badge was offered on the L461 Range Rover Sport from 2022 until the 2024 model year update. If your L461 is a 2022–2024 petrol V8 (not the P400 straight-six), it is the S68B44B. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm for free.

mainEntity.name: Has the Range Rover Sport P530 been recalled, and should I be concerned?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — a recall was issued for certain 2022 models due to a missing or incorrectly installed turbo oil drain pipe gasket. A missing or miss-fitted gasket may lead to an oil leak, which in contact with the exhaust manifold can result in a fire. Land Rover wasn't aware of any fires or injuries at the time, but it's essential to check your vehicle's recall status.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Range Rover Sport P530 Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: Most owners — best cost/warranty balance for this BMW V8

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the L461 P530 S68B44B era (2022–2024). This engine was offered for a limited period, so availability may vary.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a P530 engine typically takes 14–20 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). The 'Hot V' design and BMW-sourced engine add complexity. [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

problems.title: 'Hot V' Cooling System & Coolant Hose Failure (N63)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, overheating warnings, steam from engine bay, sweet smell, brittle hoses showing signs of cracking, low coolant warning.
problems.repairCost: £500–£1,500 (coolant hose replacement, water pump, or thermostat) [PLACEHOLDER]
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless overheating leads to engine damage)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if coolant loss is sudden or overheating occurs, stop driving immediately

problems.recommendation: The N63's "Hot V" design — with turbochargers nestled between the cylinder banks — generates extreme heat that can cause coolant hoses to become brittle and fail prematurely. The coolant system works harder than in a conventional V8 layout, and any failure can lead to rapid overheating and catastrophic engine damage. Regular inspection of all coolant hoses, particularly those near the turbochargers, is essential. If your P530 hasn't had a cooling system inspection, consider it preventative maintenance.

problems.title: Turbo Oil Drain Pipe Gasket Failure (Recall Issue)
problems.symptoms: Oil leak from the turbo area, burning oil smell, visible oil under the vehicle, smoke from engine bay, potential fire risk.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless oil leak leads to fire or turbo damage)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you notice any oil leak or smell, have it inspected immediately

problems.recommendation: Land Rover issued a recall for certain 2022 Range Rover models with the BMW V8 due to a missing or incorrectly installed turbo oil drain pipe gasket. A missing or miss-fitted gasket may lead to an oil leak, which — in contact with the exhaust manifold — can result in a fire. Land Rover wasn't aware of any fires or injuries at the time of the recall, but this is a serious safety concern. Check if your vehicle's VIN is affected by the recall, and if any oil leak is present, have it inspected immediately.

problems.title: Engine Mount Failure & Rough Idle
problems.symptoms: Excessive vibration at idle, shaking felt through the cabin, clunking noises, rough idle that smooths out at higher RPM.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; can cause additional wear on other components

problems.recommendation: Several P530 owners have reported significant shaking at idle that was traced to failed engine mounts. The high torque output (750Nm) of the BMW V8 places considerable stress on the engine mounts. If you notice excessive vibration at idle, have the engine mounts inspected — replacement is relatively straightforward but can be costly due to the V8's weight and the tight engine bay.

problems.title: MHEV System & Starting Issues
problems.symptoms: Car fails to start, intermittent starting issues, battery warnings, stop-start system failure, electrical gremlins.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if starting issues develop

problems.recommendation: Some P530 owners have reported their vehicles being undriveable for weeks due to starting issues. The P530 features a mild-hybrid (MHEV) system with a 48V battery and belt-integrated starter-generator. Complex electrical systems can be difficult to diagnose. If your P530 develops starting issues, get it to a Land Rover specialist with the correct diagnostic equipment — generic scanners may not be able to read the BMW-based systems correctly.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover Sport P530 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Hose replacement is straightforward; catch it before overheating
Essential safety repair; check recall status immediately
Replacement restores smooth idle; worth doing
Requires specialist diagnostics; can be complex
The S68B44B is a new engine; catastrophic failures are extremely rare

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Range Rover Sport P530? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with oil changes at 8,000–10,000 mile intervals
* Cooling system inspected and no coolant loss history
* Recall work completed (turbo oil drain pipe gasket)
* Engine mounts inspected and no excessive vibration at idle
avoidIf: * No proof of recall work completed — this is a safety issue
* Coolant loss with no diagnosis — 'Hot V' cooling system repairs can be expensive
* Excessive vibration at idle — engine mounts may be failing
* Any starting issues reported — MHEV system problems can be complex and costly

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Range Rover Sport P530 represents a significant shift for Land Rover — trading the old 5.0-litre supercharged V8 for a BMW-sourced 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8. The result is 530PS, 750Nm of torque, and a 0-62mph time of 4.4 seconds. The S68B44B is a sophisticated, high-performance engine that brings BMW's engineering excellence to the Range Rover Sport. However, the 'Hot V' design means cooling system vigilance is essential, and the recall for the turbo oil drain pipe gasket is a safety-critical item to check. Buy on service history, ensure all recalls are completed, and this is one of the most thrilling SUVs on the road. Neglect the cooling system, and you'll face expensive repairs.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover Sport P530 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover Sport P530 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Coolant System — Check for any coolant loss; 'Hot V' hoses are a known weak point
* Recall Status — Has the turbo oil drain pipe gasket recall been completed?
* Service History — Oil changes every 8,000–10,000 miles are essential for this high-performance V8
* Cold Start — Any unusual noises? The N63 should start smoothly with no rattles
* Idle Quality — Any excessive vibration? Engine mounts may be failing
* Oil Level — Check dipstick; any oil loss could indicate turbo seal issues
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? MHEV or cooling system codes are most common
* MOT History — Check for emissions failures (often related to the complex emissions system)
